questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the derivative of f(x) = x³?
questions.hint: Use the power rule: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If f(x) = 2x + 5, what is f⁻¹(x)?
questions.hint: Set y = f(x), then solve for x in terms of y

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the 15th term of the arithmetic sequence: 7, 12, 17, 22, ...
questions.hint: Identify the common difference and use the formula aₙ = a₁ + (n-1)d

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The identity sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 is true for all angles θ.
questions.hint: This is the fundamental trigonometric identity

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 5kg object experiences a net force of 20N. What is its acceleration?
questions.hint: Use Newton's Second Law: F = ma

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A wave has a frequency of 440Hz and wavelength of 0.78m. Calculate its speed.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Calculate the resistance of a conductor if 12V potential difference produces a current of 0.5A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which type of bond is formed when electrons are shared between atoms?
questions.hint: Think about electron sharing between non-metals

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the exothermic reaction N₂ + 3H₂ ⇌ 2NH₃ + heat, which change will shift equilibrium to the right?
questions.hint: For exothermic reactions, lowering temperature favors the forward reaction

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Balance the following redox reaction in acidic solution: MnO₄⁻ + Fe²⁺ → Mn²⁺ + Fe³⁺

questions.subject: Life Sciences
questions.question: Which organelle is known as the 'powerhouse' of the cell?
questions.hint: This organelle produces ATP through cellular respiration

questions.subject: Life Sciences
questions.question: What is the complementary base pair for Adenine (A) in DNA?
questions.hint: A always pairs with T in DNA

questions.subject: Life Sciences
questions.question: Match the male reproductive structures with their functions:
questions.hint: Review the functions of each reproductive organ

questions.subject: Life Sciences
questions.question: Natural selection always favors the strongest individuals in a population.
questions.hint: Natural selection favors traits that improve survival and reproduction

questions.subject: Geography
questions.question: Which type of weathering is most common in arid (dry) regions?
questions.hint: In dry regions, temperature changes cause physical breakdown of rocks

questions.subject: Geography
questions.question: Explain how orographic rainfall occurs.
questions.correctAnswer: Moist air is forced to rise over mountains, cools adiabatically, condenses and precipitates on the windward side. Dry air descends on the leeward side.
acceptableAnswers: Air rises over mountains, cools, condenses, precipitation occurs
Relief rainfall occurs when moist air is forced up a mountain
questions.hint: Consider what happens to air as it moves over a mountain barrier

questions.subject: Accounting
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a non-current asset?
questions.hint: Non-current assets are held for more than one year

questions.subject: Accounting
questions.question: A company sells a product at R150 per unit. Variable costs are R90 per unit and fixed costs are R30,000. Calculate the break-even quantity.
questions.hint: Break-even = Fixed Costs / (Selling Price - Variable Cost)
